first_paragraph: [Snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding "Snowboarding") was one of the competitions at the [2022 Winter Paralympics](/wiki/2022_Winter_Paralympics "2022 Winter Paralympics") in Beijing, China.[[2]](#cite_note-qualification_criteria_paralympic_org-2)[[3]](#cite_note-beijing_venues_2022-3) In total, eight medal events were held.[[2]](#cite_note-qualification_criteria_paralympic_org-2)

[Snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding "Snowboarding") was one of the competitions at the [2022 Winter Paralympics](/wiki/2022_Winter_Paralympics "2022 Winter Paralympics") in Beijing, China.[[2]](#cite_note-qualification_criteria_paralympic_org-2)[[3]](#cite_note-beijing_venues_2022-3) In total, eight medal events were held.[[2]](#cite_note-qualification_criteria_paralympic_org-2)

## Classification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2022_Winter_Paralympics&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Classification")]

Main article: [Para-snowboarding classification](/wiki/Para-snowboarding_classification "Para-snowboarding classification")

Para-snowboarding is divided into three classification categories.[[4]](#cite_note-ipc_snowboarding_classification-4)

SB-LL1
:   Athletes competing in the class SB-LL1 have significant impairment to one leg, such as [amputation](/wiki/Amputation "Amputation") above the knee, or "a significant combined impairment in two legs", affecting their balance, their board-control and their ability to navigate uneven terrain.[[4]](#cite_note-ipc_snowboarding_classification-4) Athletes with annotations will use [prostheses](/wiki/Prostheses "Prostheses") during racing.[[4]](#cite_note-ipc_snowboarding_classification-4) events for female athletes in the category SB-LL1 at the 2022 Winter Paralympics is a demonstration classification.[[5]](#cite_note-para_snowboarders_call_on_ipc_to_allow_womens_ll1_athletes_to_compete_beijing_2022_2021-5)[[6]](#cite_note-ipc-2023sports-6)

SB-LL2
:   Athletes competing in the class SB-LL2 have impairment to one or both legs "with less activity limitation", such as below-knee amputation.[[4]](#cite_note-ipc_snowboarding_classification-4)

SB-UL
:   Athletes competing in the class SB-UL have upper limb impairments, affecting balance.[[4]](#cite_note-ipc_snowboarding_classification-4) Events for female athletes in the category SB-UL at the 2022 Winter Paralympics is a demonstration classification.

SB-LL1 was removed from the programme for female athletes in June 2019 as an insufficient number of athletes competed in this class at the 2019 World Para Snowboard Championships.[[7]](#cite_note-ipc_surprised_as_huckaby_wins_court_appeal_to_compete_beijing_2022_paralympics_2022-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) In January 2022, American snowboarder [Brenna Huckaby](/wiki/Brenna_Huckaby "Brenna Huckaby"), classified as SB-LL1 snowboarder, won a court decision to allow her to compete at the 2022 Winter Paralympics.[[9]](#cite_note-para_snowboarders_too_disabled_for_games_2022-9)[[10]](#cite_note-hernandez_huckaby_lead_womens_sb_ll2_snowboard_cross_qualifying_beijing_2022-10) French snowboarder [Cécile Hernandez](/wiki/C%C3%A9cile_Hernandez "Cécile Hernandez") learned days before her competition that she was also allowed to compete.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) They competed in SB-LL2 classified events.[[13]](#cite_note-hernandez_takes_snowboard_cross_gold_beijing_2022-13)

## Medal summary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2022_Winter_Paralympics&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Medal summary")]

### Medal table

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2022_Winter_Paralympics&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Medal table")]

The ranking in the table is based on information provided by the [International Paralympic Committee](/wiki/International_Paralympic_Committee "International Paralympic Committee") (IPC) and will be consistent with IPC convention in its published medal tables. By default, the table will be ordered by the number of gold medals the athletes from a nation have won (in this context, a "nation" is an entity represented by a National Paralympic Committee). The number of silver medals is taken into consideration next and then the number of bronze medals. If nations are still tied, equal ranking is given and they are listed alphabetically by IPC country code.

  \*   Host nation ([China](/wiki/China_at_the_2022_Winter_Paralympics "China at the 2022 Winter Paralympics"))
